decisions.question: Override execution model: when a GateOverrideRecorded event exists for a gate in the current epoch, does the advance loop (a) skip gate execution entirely and use override data directly, or (b) execute the gate first to capture actual output for the audit trail, then substitute with override data? The PRD says GateOverrideRecorded captures 'actual gate output' -- but option (a) means no actual output exists. Option (b) means every overridden gate still runs (latency cost) but the audit trail is complete.

override_default declaration and resolution: how is override_default declared in templates (new optional field on the Gate struct in template/types.rs)? What are the built-in defaults per gate type (command -> {exit_code:0,error:''}, context-exists -> {exists:true,error:''}, context-matches -> {matches:true,error:''})? How does the advance loop resolve 'no --with-data' -- check instance default first, fall back to type default? What happens when no default exists and no --with-data is provided?
decisions.chosen: Option A: optional override_default field on Gate struct (inline, co-located). Resolution order: instance override_default > built-in type default. Built-in type defaults always exist for known gate types, so calls without --with-data never fail.

decisions.question: Override event persistence and advance loop integration: how does derive_overrides scope to the current epoch (mirror derive_decisions pattern)? How does override data enter the gates.* evidence map -- does it replace what gate evaluation produced, or is it injected when gate execution is skipped? How is any_failed computed when some gates are overridden (overridden gates with a passing override_default should clear the blocked condition)? How does agent_actionable in blocking_conditions get set true when a gate has an override_default? NOTE: this decision is downstream of D1 -- the fields available in GateOverrideRecorded depend on whether the gate ran.
decisions.chosen: GateOverrideRecorded: 6 fields (state, gate, rationale, override_applied, actual_output, timestamp). Advance loop: pre-check derive_overrides once before gate loop, split gates into overridden (inject override_applied) and live (pass to evaluate_gates). any_failed: overridden gate is unconditional pass (override event is the unblocking signal; value still flows into gates.* for routing). agent_actionable: true when gate has instance override_default OR built-in type default exists.

decisions.question: koto overrides CLI structure and derive_overrides API: how does the subcommand mirror koto decisions (Clap enum, dispatch pattern)? What is the output format of koto overrides list (array of override objects with gate, rationale, override_applied, actual_output if available, timestamp)? What is the scope of derive_overrides -- current epoch only (matching derive_decisions) or cross-epoch (all overrides in session history, as required by R8)?
decisions.chosen: Option A for CLI (separate --gate and --rationale flags per PRD R5). Two functions: derive_overrides (current epoch, mirrors derive_decisions, used by advance loop) and derive_overrides_all (cross-epoch, used by koto overrides list). Output format mirrors decisions list: {state, overrides: {count, items: [{state, gate, rationale, override_applied, actual_output, timestamp}]}}.

decisions.question: gates namespace reservation enforcement: where in the call stack are agent submissions with gates.* keys rejected? Options: (a) at the CLI layer in handle_next before appending EvidenceSubmitted, (b) in the engine's advance loop before merging evidence, (c) both. R7 says the gates key is reserved -- is this enforced at evidence submission time (koto next --with-data) only, or also at context submission (koto context set)?
decisions.chosen: Option C: both CLI and engine layers. CLI layer (handle_next) checks for 'gates' top-level key before validate_evidence, returns InvalidSubmission with clear message. Engine layer converts the existing silent overwrite to an explicit assertion (warning/debug panic). Context store (koto context set) is a separate namespace with no collision risk -- no reservation needed there.
